_meta.description: Per-family RAM-per-vCPU ratios and parsing rules for Azure VM SKUs. Azure's pricing API does not carry vCPU or RAM on priced rows; we derive both from armSkuName via the documented naming convention (https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/vm-naming-conventions). vCPU is parsed from the SKU name. RAM is computed as `vcpu * ram_per_vcpu_gb` with optional adjustments via modifiers (h doubles RAM, l halves it).

schema.<family>.ram_per_vcpu_gb: Default GB of RAM per vCPU for this family generation.
schema.<family>.family_slug: Maps to a taxonomy family slug from families.json.
schema.<family>.modifiers: Per-letter adjustments applied to the base spec. 'h' = high memory (typically 2x), 'l' = low memory (typically 0.5x). Other letters in the SKU (a/s/d/i/p/t/c) are flavor markers and do not change vCPU or RAM.
schema.<family>.notes: Free-text architecture context for agent prose.

_meta.v0_coverage: v0 covers the modern D, E, F, L families with v3 to v6 generations under Linux OnDemand pricing. M-series (large RAM), N-series (GPU), H-series (HPC), A-series (legacy), B-series (burstable), G/GS (deprecated) are out of v0 for now. SKUs in those families appear as filtered_skipped in the index report, not unclassified, because the absence is deliberate.

D.notes: Balanced general-purpose. Dsv5 = Intel Ice Lake, Dasv5 = AMD Milan, Dadsv6 = AMD Genoa.

E.notes: Memory-optimized 1:8 vCPU:GB ratio. Esv5 = Ice Lake, Easv5 = Milan, Eadsv7 = Genoa.

F.notes: Compute-optimized 1:2 vCPU:GB ratio. Fsv2 = Cascade Lake, Falsv6 = AMD Genoa.

L.notes: Storage-optimized with local NVMe. Lsv3 = Ice Lake, Lasv4 = AMD.
